title: Bangladesh Summons Indian Envoy After PM's Adviser Held at Delhi Airport for Two Hours
summary: Bangladesh policy adviser Zahed ur Rahman was detained without explanation at IGI Airport for over two hours during a scheduled IORA conference visit, forcing him to abandon the trip. Dhaka subsequently summoned India's acting deputy high commissioner — the latest flashpoint in a relationship already strained since the political transition in Dhaka.

coverages.headline: Bangladesh summons Indian envoy after PM's adviser held at Delhi airport for 2+ hours
coverages.excerpt: Bangladesh policy adviser Zahed ur Rahman was detained without explanation at IGI Airport during a scheduled IORA conference visit, forcing him to abandon the trip; Dhaka subsequently summoned India's acting deputy high commissioner.
coverages.bias_notes: The Wire is the only outlet with confirmed coverage of this incident today, which limits the ability to compare framing across the spectrum. The piece reports the detention as unexplained — a notable gap, since no Indian government justification is cited or appears to have been offered. Read alongside the ongoing trust deficit story between India and Bangladesh, this incident fits a pattern of low-level diplomatic friction accumulating without either side issuing a clear public accounting. The absence of an Indian official response in the piece may reflect The Wire's sourcing limitations as much as government silence — worth verifying against other outlets as they publish.
